item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No problem! I've been on and off here as well so I haven't been able to reply much.

I honestly like the air of mystery and incomplete information on just what is happening. It gives just enough information to imply that something is terribly, terribly wrong with time in the SCP's original universe, and honestly I've always enjoyed the type of horror involved in reality and time itself breaking apart around you even as you struggle to survive. Anyways, kudos on the entry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for the very, very late reply.

For the most part yes. For the sake of better mystery, I opted to not explain what happened in regards to the temporal shenanigans, even to myself. But the rest was spot-on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I never got to see the coldposted article this was based on, but my theory is that the PT boat was involved in some sort of alternate-history WWII, where an invasion of mainland Japan (Operation Typhoon) occurred, but Japan, or the Allies, used some sort of negative-temporal-wedgie superweapon that apparently broke time. Would I be accurate?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. This had some more mystery to it than some of the entries in the murder mystery section of the Jam contest. I'm guessing, based on the changes that went down, something happened that stranded the ship, and a mutiny of some kind took place?

Not sure what the obsession with time and clocks are though, but it does provide an intriguing mystery.

Either way, this a solid SCP article, tretter. +1.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Alright, this was inspired by a coldposted article that was written, then deleted almost a year ago. Thanks to Shaggy, Cimmerian, Stallmantic, and Not_A_Seagull in chat for looking at this.
